codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers all expected behaviors: max turns limit, wall-clock timeout, token/cost caps, and failure threshold tracking. 18 unit tests all pass. Code is idiomatic Rust with proper use of thiserror, async tests with tokio, and builder pattern for SessionLimits configuration.

issues.description: warning_ratio is hardcoded to 0.75 and not configurable via SessionLimits. This is a minor limitation - the warning threshold could be made configurable if needed in the future.

Cost comparison in check_limits() converts f64 to u64 (cents) for integer comparison. This is a reasonable approach but could theoretically have precision issues if cost_used is NaN or Infinity. However, cost values from LLM APIs should always be valid positive numbers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed project conventions properly: used thiserror::Error for error types, #[tokio::test] for async tests, proper module organization with lib.rs exports, and followed the builder pattern for SessionLimits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ResourceLimitError uses #[derive(thiserror::Error)], async tests use #[tokio::test], SessionLimits uses with_* builder pattern consistent with other configuration types in the codebase.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill guidance. The skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure: true with no deviations. No issues with ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, or error handling patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read skill files, explored existing types in swell-core (cost_tracking.rs, kill_switch.rs), and properly integrated with CostRecord and CostSummary from swell_core::cost_tracking.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services were started by the worker. Only standard cargo commands were used for build/test/lint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands run: cargo build --workspace, cargo clippy -p swell-tools, cargo test -p swell-tools resource_limits. No services.yaml updates needed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered and properly used swell_core::cost_tracking::{CostRecord, CostSummary} for integration with existing cost tracking infrastructure. This integration is appropriate and follows the codebase patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SessionResourceTracker has record_cost_record() and record_cost_summary() methods that accept swell_core types. The cost tracking types are already properly defined in swell-core.

summary: Feature implementation is complete and correct. All 4 expected behaviors (max turns, wall-clock timeout, token/cost caps, failure tracking) are implemented with 18 passing unit tests. No blocking issues found. Worker followed Rust skill and project conventions properly.
